citation: April Showers Bring Superpowers: A Crowd-Sourced Media Recommendation List by FMRT, GameRT, and GNCRT (2023)
summary:
# Summary

The provided text is an informational document titled "April Showers Bring Superpowers," dated 2023, associated with the American Library Association (ALA). It functions as a promotional schedule and resource guide for a virtual event hosted collaboratively by three ALA Round Tables: Films & Media (FMRT), Games & Gaming (GameRT), and Graphic Novels & Comics (GNCRT). The document does not present original empirical research, statistical analysis, or theoretical arguments. Instead, it serves as a crowd-sourced compilation of media recommendations centered on the theme of "superheroes," intended for librarians and library professionals interested in these genres.

The primary purpose of the text is to outline the agenda for an event scheduled for Friday, April 28, 2023, which was broadcast via Twitch. The document explicitly states that the media list is "crowd-sourced" from members of the three round tables. It includes detailed presenter biographies for key figures such as Danielle Costello (LSU), Liz Brown (UCCS), Kit Steinkellner, and Sebastian Kadlecik, who are associated with specific segments like a comics reading of *Quince* by Fanbase Press. The text also provides logistical details, including Twitch links, a Zoom link for audience chat, and an Airtable survey for prize entry.

The core content consists of categorized lists of recommended media. These categories include Books (e.g., *Cute Mutants*, *Dreadnought*), Comics (e.g., *Batman: The Court of Owls*, *She-Hulk*), Films & TV (e.g., *Blue Beetle*, *Loki*, *Moon Knight*), and Games. The gaming section is further subdivided into Board & Card Games (e.g., *Marvel Champions*, *Sentinels of the Multiverse*), Tabletop Role-Playing Games (TTRPGs) (e.g., *City of Mist*, *Masks*), and Video Games (e.g., *Marvel’s Spider-Man*). Additionally, an "Other Content" section lists podcasts, TikTok videos, and articles, reflecting a broad interpretation of media engagement. A distinct section titled "ChatGPT Information" lists resources on AI in higher education, such as "AI In Higher Ed: An Introduction without the Hype," indicating a concurrent interest in artificial intelligence literacy within these professional circles.

From the perspective of Games-Based Library Services (GBLS), this document is relevant as it demonstrates active curation and promotion of game-related media by library professionals. The inclusion of TTRPGs, board games, and video games alongside traditional print media highlights the interdisciplinary nature of modern library services. The document underscores the role of ALA Round Tables in facilitating community building and resource sharing among librarians specializing in niche media formats. However, as it is an event schedule and recommendation list rather than a research article, it offers no data on user engagement, learning outcomes, or the efficacy of games-based services. It serves primarily as evidence of professional interest and curatorial practice within the library community regarding superhero-themed media and gaming. The text concludes with contact information and links for joining the round tables, reinforcing its function as a recruitment and engagement tool for ALA members.
sourceText:
April Showers Bring Superpowers
This list is a crowd-sourced document of favorite media recommended by members of the
FMRT (Films & Media Round Table) GameRT (Games & Gaming Round Table) and the GNCRT (Graphic Novels & Comics Round Table) of the ALA (American Library Association).
You can find information about joining the round tables on their websites and apply for membership here.
All programming will be broadcast via Twitch. Tune in here: https://www.twitch.tv/alagamert
Recording collection for this event on Twitch: https://www.twitch.tv/collections/lk_hCoZDIBdLrA
Fill out our survey to win some spectacular prizes: https://airtable.com/shrggxxkT3xen6KmD
#ALAcapesandcarts23

GameRT Twitch Policy

GNCRT

2

GameRT Twitch Policy
1. Be kind and respectful to one another. 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated. 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason. 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch
mod and one of us will assist you. 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct
message.
Schedule of events for Friday, April 28, 2023
🥐11:00 - 11:45 Round Table Round Table
Batman Arkham Asylum Batman: the Animated Series Batman: Arkham VR The Boys, Garth Ennis & Darik Robertson Hawkeye: My Life as a Weapon, Matt Fraction & David Aja (2012) Invincible, Robert Kirkman, Bill Crabtree, & Cory Walker (2023) Irredeemable, Mark Wade, Peter Krause, Anderew Dalhouse, & Ed Dukeshire (2015) Jessica Jones, Netflix series Marvel Snap Moon Knight: the Bottom, Charlie Huston & David Finch (2007) Ms. Marvel, G Willow Wilson, Adrian Alphona, & Sara Pichelli (2014) Superman: Red Son, Mark Millar, Andrew Robinson, Dave Johnson, Kilian Plunkett, & Walden Wong (2004) Spiderman: Into the Spiderverse, Sony Pictures, 2019 The Unbeatable Squirrel Girl, Ryan North, Erica Henderson (2015) Xmen: the Animated Series
Presenter bios:
Host: Danielle Costello, dcostello@lsu.edu Presenters: Liz Brown, ebrown22@uccs.edu
12:00 - 12:45 GNCRT: Comics Reading
Quince Creator: Sebastian Kadlecik Writer: Kit Steinkellner Artist: Emma Steinkellner Translator: Valeria Tranier

Formatting: Oceano Ransford Publisher: Fanbase Press
Presenter bios: Kit Steinkellner, @BooksAreMyBFs Kit Steinkellner is a film and television writer best known as the creator and executive producer of the Facebook Watch half-hour series, Sorry for Your Loss, which holds a 97% "Certified Fresh" score on Rotten Tomatoes As a television writer, Steinkellner recently created and showran her pilot, The Answers, for FX, is currently developing for Hulu and Starz, and has penned feature scripts for Netflix, Sony, and Lionsgate.
Sebastian Kadlecik, @smkaddy Sebastian Martinez-Kadlecik is a writer, illustrator, and actor. He’s written everything from sketch comedy to spoken word poetry, provided artwork for numerous television shows and indie comics far and wide, and acted in a few indie films while doing tons of theatre. He is the creator of the epic interspecies saga, Penguins vs. Possums, for which he was also a writer, illustrator, and letterer. Quince is a uniquely personal project, and he’s so happy to have taken the journey with Kit, Emma, and Fanbase Press. For Sarita, Olivia, Max, Mom, toda la familia, mi corazón Shirley, y nuestro Samuelito.
Fanbase Press’ Social Media Facebook: FanbasePress1 Twitter: @Fanbase_Press Instagram: @fanbasepress
1:00 - 1:30 Audience Chat Zoom link
1:45 - 2:45 FMRT: "ChatGPT Unleashed: Crafting Superhero Blockbusters Through AI-Powered Scripts"
